title: nvm_list cursor advance uses out_id not out_id+1
description: In nvm_list(), after each wh_Client_NvmList call the cursor (start_id) is set to out_id, not out_id+1. The wolfHSM server (wh_NvmFlash_List, wh_NvmFlashLog_List) performs an exact-match lookup on start_id and returns the *next* object after the matched entry. Incrementing start_id to out_id+1 would cause spurious WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND for non-contiguous ID spaces because there is no guarantee that out_id+1 exists as a stored object. This was confirmed against the wolfHSM C source and upstream test suite (wh_test_clientserver.c). A contributor might 'simplify' this to out_id+1 imagining a lower-bound search semantics; that is wrong. The comment block in the code and this decision record both explain the exact-match contract.
